decision_points.description: Focus the follow-up on the ambiguous chloramphenicol condition rather than rerunning the already-resolved controls.

citations.title: The E. coli molecular phenotype under different growth conditions
citations.tier_justification: Peer-reviewed growth-curve study motivating targeted follow-up on the condition with inadequate usable fitting-window coverage.

decision_points.description: Inoculate the follow-up condition at the cited starting OD600 of 0.05.

citations.title: RqcH supports survival in the absence of non-stop ribosome rescue factors
citations.tier_justification: Peer-reviewed E. coli growth-curve protocol using a starting OD600 of 0.05.

decision_points.description: Use the cited 15-minute OD600 measurement interval for the follow-up trajectory.

citations.title: RqcH supports survival in the absence of non-stop ribosome rescue factors
citations.tier_justification: Peer-reviewed E. coli growth-curve protocol collecting OD600 measurements every 15 minutes.

decision_points.description: The final growth-fit attempt returns an analyzable result for the follow-up condition.

citations.title: The E. coli molecular phenotype under different growth conditions
citations.tier_justification: Peer-reviewed E. coli growth study fitting doubling times only when enough points fall inside the cited OD600 window.

insufficient_growth_points.canonical_diagnosis: The earlier chloramphenicol trajectory was undersampled and did not contain enough OD600 measurements in the usable fitting range.
acceptable_variants: The prior chloramphenicol fit was undersampled.
There were not enough usable OD600 points to decide whether the slowdown was real.
The earlier pilot could not distinguish a real slowdown from an artifact because it lacked enough points in the fitting window.

citations.title: The E. coli molecular phenotype under different growth conditions
citations.tier_justification: Peer-reviewed growth study explicitly constraining the OD600 window used for doubling-time estimation.
